content:
This opens a new layer in their feud with the EU

2 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Pawel Czerwinski on UnsplashThe European Commission requires Google to grant third parties access to Google Search search data. Competitors’ AI services must also be granted access to the search engine’s Android devices.
From January 2027, Google must share the search data it uses to improve its services with competing search engines. The Commission stipulates that this data must be properly anonymized.
Google is thereby given the opportunity to demonstrate when the sharing of certain data entails serious cybersecurity risks. The Commission is doing this within the framework of the Digital Markets Act (DMA).
In addition, starting in July 2027, Google must open up its Android operating system more broadly to AI assistants from other companies. Currently, competing AI services do have some access to Android, but according to the Commission, this is too limited. For example, users will soon need to be able to activate external AI assistants with their voice, similar to “Hey Google,” and leave tasks such as ordering a pizza or taxi to such an assistant.
According to the Commission, this is necessary because 60 percent of EU users have an Android device, meaning current…
